variant_label: Demographics & Population — Iran population when the Shah fell
notes: Iran's population was 37 million when the Shah fell (1978/79), used as the baseline for the war-era population surge. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.iv]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Iran population, midyear 1988
notes: Rapid population growth swelled Iran's population from about 37 million (1978) to 52 million by midyear 1988. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.iv]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Iran population increase, 1980-1986
notes: Iran's population grew by near 20 percent between 1980 and 1986, the denominator against which the real import-cut burden is measured. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.1]

variant_label: Social Protection — Iran unemployment insurance, maximum monthly payment
notes: In August 1987, Iran's Ministry of Labor established unemployment insurance entitling some laid-off employees to monthly payments of up to $575. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Social Protection — Mosque-affiliated community bank loan ceiling
notes: Small community banks associated with mosques ("Funds for the Lending of Money for Good Purposes") sprang up nationwide, offering loans of up to $30,000 with minimal red tape. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Oil sector share of Iran GNP, revolution-era peak
notes: Iran's oil sector has shrunk from at least 38 percent of GNP at the beginning of the revolution to less than 10 percent in 1987. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Oil sector share of Iran GNP, 1987
notes: By 1987 the oil sector had fallen to less than 10 percent of Iran's GNP, reflecting falling oil export revenues rather than nonoil sector gains. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Oil production share of Iraq GNP, prewar
notes: Oil production accounted for about 50 percent of Iraq's GNP in 1979-80, before the war. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.5]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Oil production share of Iraq GNP, 1987
notes: By 1987 oil production accounted for roughly 40 percent of Iraq's GNP, reflecting reduced oil production and lower prices rather than real nonoil-sector gains. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.5]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Iran population growth rate, recent years average
notes: Iran's population growth rate has averaged at least 3.5 percent annually in recent years, per Iranian and Western demographers. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Iran projected to join 100-million-population club
notes: At its then-current population growth rate, Iran was projected to be one of about 15 countries with populations of at least 100 million within roughly 20 years (i.e. around 2008). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Tehran metro-area population growth since the revolution
notes: Natural growth plus rural-to-urban migration roughly doubled the Tehran area's population -- to about 10 million -- since the revolution. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Health — Iran doctor shortage vs. Shah-era level
notes: As of 1985, Iran had lost at least half of the 15,000 doctors it had under the Shah (many Western-educated physicians left after the revolution or retired early to avoid war-front service). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Health — Iran doctor count under the Shah (baseline)
notes: Baseline: Iran had 15,000 doctors under the Shah; by 1985 at least half had been lost. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Iraq population before the war
notes: Iraq's population grew from about 13 million before the war to about 18 million in 1987. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Iraq population, 1987
notes: Iraq's population reached about 18 million in 1987 (compare Table 1's midyear-1988 figure of 17.6 million). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Iraq fertility rate
notes: Iraq's high fertility rate -- nearly 7 percent -- and youthful population indicate continued high population growth (note: source states 'percent' for what is likely a loosely-used fertility-rate figure, transcribed as printed). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Iran investment needed to restore per-capita GNP to 10-years-earlier level
notes: The Iranian economy needs tens of billions of dollars of investment to return its per capita gross national product to levels achieved 10 years earlier (i.e. pre-revolution/pre-war). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 88-10067, "Iran-Iraq: A Comparison of Two War-Weary Economies" (An Intelligence Assessment, information as of 10 Nov 1988), November 1988, PDF p.19]
